behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is string segmentation logic at one function/object boundary. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: no level lower than Unit exists. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text is only about segmentation output, not cross-module handoff or filesystem/process effects.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a multi-line shell snippet with assignments and a command into the segmenter and assert it returns distinct segments per newline statement (including preserving command boundaries), instead of one combined segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: incident failure appears when segmentation output is handed to trigger classification; that module interaction is the boundary. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: a Unit test of trigger matcher alone could pass while the composed pipeline still labels the full block `not_a_trigger` due to segmentation/classifier handoff. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text only concerns local hook parsing/classification of command text.
behaviors.test_description: Run the post-tool classification pipeline end-to-end in-process with an input block containing newline-separated assignments then `gh pr create`; assert final classification is PR-create trigger (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure risk is again at segmentation + trigger-rule composition, but for negative discrimination. REAL-INFRA: no. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit-testing only the PR regex could miss a pipeline bug where prior segment context or broad matcher causes false PR-trigger classification. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is local command interpretation, no real external dependency or model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the same in-process post-tool parsing/classification flow on a multi-line block ending in `gh issue create`; assert it is not classified as PR-create trigger.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires detector/extractor plus state persistence wiring. REAL-INFRA: local filesystem persistence is sufficient; no OS-specific primitive beyond normal file I/O. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit tests for ID reconstruction could pass while persistence adapter writes wrong field/key/path, causing missing PR identifier in saved state. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text does not require real subprocess/network; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI decision chain.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp workspace, feed a detected PR-create event through hook logic, then assert persisted review-loop state contains the expected reconstructed PR identifier at the contract-defined field.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is an inter-module contract (writer hook -> reader/enforcer hook) with shared state schema. REAL-INFRA: local file read/write in-process temp dir is enough; no requirement for real network/subprocess/OS event semantics. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no LLM-based decisions. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit tests of writer alone would miss a schema/version/key mismatch that causes pre-tool hook to ignore state and fail to enforce. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text asks for read/enforce compatibility, not OS-specific behavior under real external infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Run post-tool state write path, then invoke pre-tool enforcement reader against the emitted state and assert enforcement activates based on that state (contract compatibility test across both hooks).
